feedback.text: I think we need to sort out digital ownership, especially for things like video games and eBooks. Right now, it often feels like you're just renting them. You might buy a game, but if the online store removes it or the publisher shuts down the servers, you can lose access to a game you paid for. I get that companies can't support games forever, but they should give players the tools to keep playing when they're done, like letting us keep playing it offline or host our own servers when needed. It's the same with eBooks, a book can just disappear from your device, and its getting harder to download your own files for safekeeping. Simply put, if you buy something digital, you should actually own it, just like a physical book or game disc.
